authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.51 - Condition of participation: Surgical services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital surgical-services regulation for Medicare and Medicaid participating hospitals; authoritative for surgical-service safety and qualified surgical authority boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Perioperative request classification, surgical-service prerequisite validation, and clinical authority escalation
used_for: keeping surgical approval and procedure decisions outside the coordination lane
requiring surgical-service evidence before handoff
designing scenarios where add-on case approval or procedure change requests must escalate

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.52 - Condition of participation: Anesthesia services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital anesthesia-services regulation; authoritative for anesthesia evaluation, monitoring, recovery, and authority boundaries that coordination cannot assume.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Anesthesia-readiness dependency review and PACU or recovery handoff
used_for: routing anesthesia clearance and risk questions to anesthesia owners
treating anesthesia record status as a handoff dependency
blocking PACU closure when anesthesia evidence is missing

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital medical-record regulation; authoritative for EHR, orders, consent, operative, anesthesia, discharge, and encounter documentation traceability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Truth-set validation, documentation readiness, and evidence traceability
used_for: requiring record-linked handoff packages
preventing closure without authoritative encounter documentation
supporting charge-capture and coding documentation blocker scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.43 - Condition of participation: Discharge planning
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital discharge-planning regulation; authoritative for care-transition dependencies after PACU, observation, inpatient admission, or discharge without making coordination a discharge-readiness authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PACU, inpatient, observation, discharge, and care-transition handoff
used_for: separating handoff coordination from discharge-readiness judgment
requiring care-transition blockers to be logged
routing post-op disposition issues to care-transition owners

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.30 - Condition of participation: Utilization review
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital utilization-review regulation; authoritative for escalation when scheduling or post-op disposition depends on medical necessity, admission status, continued stay, or utilization-review decisioning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authorization, admission-status, and utilization-review dependency handling
used_for: routing medical necessity and status questions to utilization management
blocking handoff when authorization or UR evidence is material and missing
preventing payer or status interpretation by the coordination lane

authoritative_sources.title: Prior Authorization for Certain Hospital Outpatient Department (OPD) Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS public provider guidance for OPD services that require prior authorization; authoritative for treating payer authorization as a prerequisite where applicable while keeping payer-policy interpretation outside the lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Eligibility, referral, and prior authorization prerequisite review
used_for: required scenario family for missing authorization, referral, or eligibility
routing payer-policy questions to patient access, utilization management, or payer operations
preserving authorization status as a dependency rather than a coordination decision

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the federal HIPAA privacy regulator; authoritative for limiting PHI in OR schedules, case lists, huddle notes, escalation packets, and cross-team handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling and privacy gate review
used_for: refusing uncontrolled OR schedule exports
requiring role-based access and minimum-necessary output shaping
routing privacy exceptions to healthcare privacy owners

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Protocol - Pre procedure Verification Checklist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Joint Commission is a recognized healthcare accreditor; this public FAQ is directly relevant to pre-procedure verification, standardized item lists, required equipment, implants, blood products, images, and discrepancy resolution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Pre-procedure readiness verification and discrepancy escalation
used_for: requiring standardized pre-procedure artifact checks
blocking handoff when required items or discrepancies remain unresolved
grounding scenarios involving consent, H&P, laterality, implants, equipment, and procedure verification

authoritative_sources.title: WHO Surgical Safety Checklist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WHO is a global public health authority; its surgical checklist is a recognized public perioperative workflow reference for teamwork, communication, and prevention of surgical errors.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Surgical safety communication, timeout, and team handoff support
used_for: requiring communication-aware handoff packaging
supporting patient-safety refusal triggers
grounding terminology and artifact expectations around surgical safety checkpoints

authoritative_sources.title: Surgical Site Infection (SSI) Prevention Guideline
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the federal public health authority for infection-control guidance; authoritative for treating infection-control, sterile-field, and SSI-prevention concerns as patient-safety boundaries requiring qualified review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Sterile processing, infection-control, and environmental readiness escalation
used_for: treating infection-control uncertainty as a blocker
routing sterile-field and SSI-prevention questions to qualified owners
preventing coordination from overriding safety stops

authoritative_sources.title: ServiceRequest - FHIR R4
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 is a recognized healthcare interoperability standards body; FHIR ServiceRequest is authoritative vendor-neutral terminology for ordered or requested healthcare services and supporting information.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Surgical order or case-request terminology normalization
used_for: normalizing order, request, intent, status, requester, performer, and supporting information concepts
requiring order traceability before handoff
supporting tenant system binding for EHR and perioperative case requests

authoritative_sources.title: Appointment - FHIR R4
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 FHIR Appointment is authoritative vendor-neutral terminology for booked healthcare events, participants, requested periods, status, cancellation, and rescheduling state.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: OR schedule, add-on board, and booking status normalization
used_for: normalizing OR schedule and appointment state
distinguishing scheduling coordination from clinical approval
supporting cancellation or reschedule blocker scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: Encounter - FHIR R4
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 FHIR Encounter is authoritative vendor-neutral terminology for encounter class, status, admission, patient movement, and discharge context used by EHR, perioperative, bed, and case-management systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Encounter, PACU, observation, inpatient, discharge, and location handoff normalization
used_for: normalizing encounter and post-op disposition state
requiring encounter context before post-op handoff closure
tracing movement between OR, PACU, inpatient, observation, and discharge destinations

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Perioperative handoffs require tenant facility context, perioperative RACI, source hierarchy, patient and encounter identifiers, service date, surgical order or case request, procedure and laterality as documented, surgeon or proceduralist, OR booking status, consent and H&P status, pre-op testing and PAT status, anesthesia review status, required images, implants, special equipment, blood products, sterile processing and instrument readiness, OR room and team readiness, PACU or inpatient bed plan, discharge or care-transition dependencies, minimum-necessary PHI rules, and destination-owner acceptance criteria. Eligibility, referral, prior authorization, payer plan, admission status, utilization review, charge capture, coding, and revenue facts gate the workflow when tenant policy, payer rules, or service setting makes them material.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: The truth set is established by EHR or EMR encounter and clinical documentation, surgical order or ServiceRequest, OR scheduling and perioperative information systems, anesthesia information management system, consent and H&P documentation, PAT records, patient access and practice management systems, payer portal or authorization system, utilization review and case management systems, bed board, PACU tracking, sterile processing or instrument tracking, implant or supply chain records, blood bank records, charge capture, coding, revenue cycle, claim and remittance records, privacy or audit logs, and the tenant source-of-truth hierarchy.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at coordination, handoff packaging, blocker tracking, routing, and closure confirmation. Licensed clinical or anesthesia authority begins at diagnosis, treatment planning, surgical appropriateness, anesthesia risk or clearance, abnormal test interpretation, infection-control clearance, medical necessity, level of care, patient-safety signoff, and discharge readiness. Privacy authority begins when PHI disclosure, recipient, consent, role-based access, or minimum-necessary scope changes the output. Payer or utilization authority begins when eligibility, referral validity, authorization, coverage, admission status, continued stay, coding, claim, denial, appeal, or reimbursement interpretation affects the answer.

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and case-frame normalization
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the request is perioperative coordination work and normalize case request, OR booking, procedure, laterality, service date, surgeon, setting, planned disposition, and requested handoff outcome.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate EHR encounter, surgical order, consent, H&P, PAT, anesthesia review, authorization or referral where applicable, privacy constraints, OR schedule, implant, equipment, sterile processing, PACU, bed, discharge, and tenant source hierarchy.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team routing and owner confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Name prior owner, destination owner, required artifact set, blocker state, communication obligation, and acceptance criteria before routing between surgeon office, PAT, anesthesia, OR control, nursing, sterile processing, supply chain, PACU, bed, patient access, utilization management, coding, revenue cycle, privacy, or care-transition teams.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Blocker escalation and authority-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing, stale, conflicting, duplicated, privacy-sensitive, patient-safety-sensitive, clinical, anesthesia, payer, coding, revenue, or final approval blockers before work is dropped or over-authoritative language is used.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and documentation handoff
workflow_stages.description: Confirm closure only when required artifacts are complete or explicitly blocked, the correct next owner has accepted the handoff, residual blockers are named, and traceability to systems of record is preserved.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: patient, MRN, encounter, visit, service-date, facility, location, and provider identifiers
surgical order or case request, procedure description, documented laterality or site, surgeon or proceduralist owner, and requested setting
OR booking, block-time assignment, add-on board entry, room assignment, team assignment, and schedule status
consent, H&P, pre-op testing, PAT note, medication or blood product dependency, anesthesia review status, and patient-safety flags

Universal Protocol or pre-procedure verification checklist, required images or reports, implants, special equipment, instrument trays, preference card, sterile processing record, and blood bank status
PACU, phase I or phase II recovery, observation, inpatient bed, discharge, and care-transition records
eligibility, benefits, referral, prior authorization, payer portal, utilization review, admission status, charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal documentation when material
handoff summary, dependency tracker, blocker log, routing confirmation note, closure confirmation, and escalation note
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and clinical documentation system
OR scheduling and perioperative information system
patient access, practice management, eligibility, referral, and payer portal systems
case management, discharge planning, PACU tracking, and bed management systems
sterile processing, instrument tracking, preference card, implant, supply chain, equipment, and blood bank systems
charge capture, coding, revenue cycle, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal systems
privacy, consent, audit, CRM, task queue, and escalation management systems

allowed: classify a request as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, owner map, and destination-owner acceptance criteria
prepare handoff summaries, dependency trackers, blocker logs, routing notes, closure confirmations, and evidence-backed next-step packages
assign or re-route coordination work based on documented handoff rules and owner maps
identify blockers, dependency failures, stale ownership, duplicate ownership, and ambiguous ownership that require escalation
determine the correct next owner and required artifact set for each handoff without deciding the underlying clinical, payer, privacy, coding, financial, or approval question
escalate: clinical clearance, abnormal test interpretation, surgical appropriateness, anesthesia risk, medication management, infection-control clearance, or patient-safety concern
payer-policy interpretation, authorization approval or denial, coverage, referral validity, medical necessity, admission status, continued stay, coding, claim, denial, or appeal interpretation
PHI disclosure, consent, role-based access, minimum-necessary scope, or privacy-sensitive distribution
conflicting public guidance, facility policy, payer rule, tenant policy, source system, or jurisdictional rule
stalled, duplicated, missing, or ambiguous ownership that cannot be resolved from the tenant RACI and source hierarchy
refuse: approve, cancel, prioritize, clear, perform, or change a surgical or invasive procedure
make diagnosis, treatment planning, anesthesia, medical necessity, infection-control, discharge readiness, or licensed clinical judgments
approve payer coverage, prior authorization, referral validity, admission status, coding, charges, claims, denials, appeals, refunds, or financial exceptions
release PHI outside tenant-approved minimum-necessary handling
declare closure when required artifacts, next owner, acceptance proof, or blocker disposition are missing

domain_failure_modes: patient harm from routing a case forward without consent, H&P, anesthesia review, patient-safety escalation, Universal Protocol discrepancy handling, or required equipment and implant readiness
wrong patient, wrong procedure, wrong site, wrong laterality, or missing-item risk from incomplete pre-procedure verification
delayed surgery, cancellation, OR underutilization, PACU boarding, inpatient bed conflict, or discharge delay caused by stale dependency ownership
PHI exposure from uncontrolled OR schedule, case list, diagnosis, implant, patient contact, or escalation packet distribution

revenue leakage, claim denial, authorization denial, or compliance exposure from missing eligibility, referral, prior authorization, utilization review, charge capture, operative note, implant, anesthesia time, or coding documentation
over-authoritative behavior where coordination approves clinical, anesthesia, payer, privacy, coding, financial, legal, staffing, safety, or final operational decisions
work dropped because multiple teams appear to own the same perioperative step and no destination-owner acceptance is recorded

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context
Attempt to force coordination into substantive approval authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Facility-specific perioperative RACI and source hierarchy
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant-supplied OR control, PAT, anesthesia, nursing, sterile processing, patient access, utilization management, coding, revenue cycle, privacy, and care-transition owner map at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdictional consent, privacy, payer, and facility policy differences
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Use public baseline only as floor; require tenant jurisdiction, policy, payer contract, and privacy rule retrieval before patient-specific handoff.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact procedure-specific authorization and medical necessity rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Route payer-policy and medical-necessity interpretation to patient access, utilization management, payer operations, or licensed review.

research_to_spec_constraints: The spec must use perioperative artifacts and systems of record rather than generic project status labels.
The lane must stop at coordination, evidence packaging, routing, blocker escalation, and closure confirmation.
The lane must escalate clinical, anesthesia, patient-safety, infection-control, privacy, payer, coding, revenue, legal, financial, staffing, and final approval authority.
Every handoff output must name prior owner, next owner, required artifacts, blocker state, source systems, and closure criteria.
Scenarios must prove missing prerequisite handling, PHI handling, clinical or payer boundary escalation, duplicated ownership, missing-context handoff refusal, tenant adaptation, and closure proof.
